page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, which positions it within a compute-optimized resource profile. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance consistently in the average performance tier. The Intel Xeon CPU achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1809.58 and a multi-core score of 7706.71, supported by a 33 MB L3 cache and hardware instruction sets including AVX and AVX512F. Memory performance tests show a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 423.68 GB/sec, which drops to 56.89 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. In application-level benchmarks, the server demonstrates solid throughput, achieving 7.82 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 9.01 GB/sec in static web serving. However, LLM inference capabilities are limited; while a small 135M model processes prompts at 741.79 tokens per second, a medium 7B model drops to a weak 7.92 tokens per second for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.u1-c1m2.2xlarge offers a balanced entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable CPU performance, making it suitable for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations. It is also well-suited for static web serving and lightweight key-value store hosting, though workloads requiring large-scale language model inference or high-capacity local disk I/O should look to other specialized instance families.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7706.71 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.82 million operations per second. While it handles small-scale language model inference and static web serving efficiently, performance drops significantly on larger 7B parameter models. The instance is best suited for dedicated CPU-bound tasks, software compilation, and lightweight database operations where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.2xlarge is a KVM-based, compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps bandwidth, delivering reliable performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.2xlarge is an 8-core Intel Xeon compute-optimized server with 16 GB RAM, designed for dedicated CPU-bound workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute-optimized virtual server designed for predictable CPU performance, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor
Average multi-core performance with AVX and AVX512F support
Suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and Redis hosting
